message:
fix(codegen): Distinguish inline vs function-call assertions for semicolon handling

Problem:
- self.assertIs() generates `try unittest.assertIs(...)` function call that needs semicolon
- self.assertEqual() generates inline `if (!cond) return error.AssertionFailed;` that doesn't
- Previous code treated ALL self.assert*() as inline statements, causing missing semicolons

Solution:
- Created whitelist of inline assertion methods (assertEqual, assertTrue, assertLess, etc.)
- Only these return early without semicolon/newline
- Others (assertIs, assertIsInstance, assertRaises) fall through to normal path and get semicolon

Files changed:
- expr_stmt.zig: lines 54-72 - replaced blanket check with explicit whitelist
- zig_builder.zig: added emitExprStmt() method (for future builder integration)

Test results:
- test_bool now compiles and runs (140/143 tests pass, 3 runtime failures)
- test_longexp passes completely
- Remaining issue: unittest.assertFalse() calls (not self.assertFalse) still missing semicolons
